subject: [PATCH v2 0/3] mm: handle device-private PMDs in walk callbacks

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer David pointed out that thp_migration_supported() is equivalent to CONFIG_TRANSPARENT_HUGEPAGE && CONFIG_ARCH_HAS_PMD_SOFTLEAVES, and suggested dropping the check and replacing VM_BUG_ON with VM_WARN_ON_ONCE.
reviews.raw_body:
thp_migration_supported() is just effectively CONFIG_TRANSPARENT_HUGEPAGE &&
CONFIG_ARCH_HAS_PMD_SOFTLEAVES.

pmd_is_migration_entry() -> softleaf_is_migration(softleaf_from_pmd(pmd)) ==
false without CONFIG_ARCH_HAS_PMD_SOFTLEAVES. ... and
CONFIG_ARCH_HAS_PMD_SOFTLEAVES is only selected if TRANSPARENT_HUGEPAGE.

While at it, can you drop the thp_migration_supported() check and turn the
VM_BUG_ON into a VM_WARN_ON_ONCE() ?

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch series is generally good but requested to cc stable mailing list in the future.
reviews.raw_body:
Otherwise, LGTM.

Reviewed-by: Zi Yan <ziy@nvidia.com>


Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

2026-07-08.patch_summary: This patch series addresses a problem where device-private PMDs (page map directories) can cause issues in various MM walk callbacks, such as VM_BUG_ON() firing or oops on bogus vmemmap dereferences. The patches modify these callbacks to handle device-private PMDs correctly by adding checks for !pmd_present() and pmd_is_device_private_entry(). This ensures that migration entries are counted properly and non-present PMDs are skipped, preventing potential issues. The changes match the PTE path and simplify the code by folding in existing VM_BUG_ON() conditions.
